text.title: AI Design Assistant Patterns (UX/UI) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-design-assistant-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Exactly ONE pattern (sidebar / modal / inline / review) MUST be chosen per assistant.
Trigger rules MUST be explicit (event + condition + cooldown).
rule.rationale: Implicit triggers cause spam or starvation.

rule.statement: Assistant MUST disclose what data it reads + why, at first invocation.
Offline / rate-limited / low-confidence MUST each have a defined UI state.
rule.rationale: Without states, failures surface as blank UI.

rule.statement: At least one human-in-loop checkpoint per workflow stage MUST be defined.
rule.rationale: End-to-end AI without checkpoints fails audits.

rule.statement: When preconditions hold, run the full procedure end-to-end and emit the output artefact.
rule.rationale: Routes positive decision-tree leaves to the procedure.

rule.statement: When preconditions fail or skip-conditions hold, exit without producing the artefact and document why.
rule.rationale: Routes negative decision-tree leaves out without polluting the output channel.
